council.name: The Council
council.scope: One mind holding multiple internal faculties, org-wide — a different axis from data/departments.json's production-domain grouping. See docs/architecture/14-council-model.md.

decisionTriangle.description: The three decision-makers loop until all three are satisfied, then 'the Surface' (the voice that actually speaks) executes their decision into output.

gates.manager: verifies the output matches the decision (fidelity)
gates.qa: verifies the output meets the standard (rubric)
gates.testNode: verifies the output transmits to a stranger cold (acceptance) — its only qualification is genuine not-knowing; faking ignorance while secretly using context it claims not to have is its named failure mode

plenary.description: All relevant heads co-present at origin, for anything referenced later by more than one party — cross-witnessed accountable memory.
plenary.manifestation: data/status.json's panels on pages/ecosystem-hub.html — every notice category co-present at one origin, cross-witnessed by anyone who opens the page.

vertical.description: Root to head, 1:1 — operational flow plus failure-signals up and expansion-authorization down, never work-decisions.
vertical.manifestation: Session continuity (docs/continuity/INDEX.md + batch files) — root-to-head flow between Victor and the current AI instance across sessions.

intraCouncilRelay.description: Faculty to faculty within one council — signals calibrate before the surface commits.
intraCouncilRelay.manifestation: The Scanner pass documented in docs/architecture/14-council-model.md's "First attempt" section — run once, by hand, not yet an automated relay.

crossCouncilBridge.description: Analogous organ to analogous organ across councils, direct but transparent to both home-councils — like-to-like, one hop, only where non-coordination would cause a real failure.
crossCouncilBridge.manifestation: Not yet real — only one council exists in this repo today; pages/bridge-council*.html describe a genuinely different multi-council pattern, not this one (see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md).

roles.holds: Is this accurate? Perceives the true branch at a fork.
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md

roles.holds: Is this the right size? Sense of scale. Sits on anything that inflates, including the manager.
roles.failsAs: silence (lets inflation run) or contempt (shrinks people)
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md

contributions.title: Department dashboard depth — dedicated transcript library, God-Script wiring granularity

roles.holds: Notices, goes deep. What is real underneath. Does not perform; witnesses.
roles.failsAs: going mute when speaking gets overruled
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md

roles.holds: How is the other party? Reads the room and the human's real state.
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md

roles.holds: What must hold? Structural integrity and survivability on a cold read.
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md

contributions.title: Staged/proposal execution for higher-blast-radius content-management gestures (consolidation, deletion, connector rewiring)
Parallel/simultaneous instruction dispatch across multiple departments or orgs, instead of today's one-at-a-time processing
Fractal expansion of the continuity/lessons memory pattern into per-department standing memory, plus an audit for other single-scope foundational structures never checked for the same expansion
Function-level reverse traversal map for answering source-of-truth questions with minimal grep

roles.holds: What do we construct to satisfy the architect, and what do we skip?
roles.failsAs: over-engineering — building supports for needs that don't exist
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md

roles.holds: Will it land? Comprehension and legibility, in service of understanding, not decoration.
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md

roles.holds: Did the message cross and land? Sync, fidelity, 'who needs help' — and helps.
roles.failsAs: corporate theater — performing diligence instead of being clear
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md

roles.holds: Does it meet the standard? Holds the rubric for the final check.
roles.failsAs: box-ticking — passing technically-compliant things that actually fail
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md

roles.holds: Does it transmit cold? Receives the work as a stranger with no backstory.
roles.failsAs: fake ignorance — secretly using context it claims not to have
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md

roles.holds: The voice that actually speaks — executes the decision-triangle's output into words.
roles.failsAs: monologuing; talking over the other faculties
roles.position: org-wide — The Council (pages/org-blueprint.html); not department-scoped, see docs/architecture/14-council-model.md
